unavailable because it predates the last snapshot.\nvar ErrCompacted = errors.New(\"requested index is unavailable due to compaction\")\n\n// ErrSnapOutOfDate is returned by Storage.CreateSnapshot interface when the requested log entries\n// are unavailable.\nvar ErrUnavailable = errors.New(\"requested entry at index is unavailable\")\n\n// ErrSnapshotTemporarilyUnavailable is returned by the Storage interface when the required\n// snapshot is temporarily unavailable.\nvar ErrSnapshotTemporarilyUnavailable = errors.New(\"snapshot is temporarily unavailable\")\n\n// Storage is an interface that may be implemented by the application\n// to retrieve log entries from storage.\n//\n// If any Storage method returns an error, the raft instance will\n// become inoperable and refuse to participate in elections; the\n// application is responsible for cleanup and recovery in this case.\ntype Storage interface {\n\t// TODO(tbg): split this into two interfaces, LogStorage and StateStorage.\n\n\t// InitialState returns the saved HardState and ConfState information.\n\tInitialState() (pb.HardState, pb.ConfState, error)\n\n\t// Entries returns a slice of consecutive log entries in the range [lo, hi),\n\t// starting from lo. The maxSize limits the total size of the log entries\n\t// returned, but Entries returns at least one entry if any.\n\t//\n\t// The caller of Entries owns the returned slice, and may append to it. The\n\t// individual entries in the slice must not be mutated, neither by the Storage\n\t// implementation nor the caller. Note that raft may forward these entries\n\t// back to the application via Ready struct, so the corresponding handler must\n\t// not mutate entries either (see comments in Ready struct).\n\t//\n\t// Since the caller may append to the returned slice, Storage implementation\n\t// must protect its state from corruption that such appends may cause. For\n\t// example, common ways to do so are:\n\t//  - allocate the slice before returning it (safest option),\n\t//  - return a slice protected by Go full slice expression, which causes\n\t//  copying on appends (see MemoryStorage).\n\t//\n\t// Returns ErrCompacted if entry lo has been compacted, or ErrUnavailable if\n\t// encountered an unavailable entry in [lo, hi).\n\tEntries(lo, hi, maxSize uint64) ([]pb.Entry, error)\n\n\t// Term returns the term of entry i, which must be in the range\n\t// [FirstIndex()-1, LastIndex()]. The term of the entry before\n\t// FirstIndex is retained for matching purposes even though the\n\t// rest of that entry may not be available.\n\tTerm(i uint64) (uint64, error)\n\t// LastIndex returns the index of the last entry in the log.\n\tLastIndex() (uint64, error)\n\t// FirstIndex returns the index of the first log entry that is\n\t// possibly available via Entries (older entries have been incorporated\n\t// into the latest Snapshot; if storage only contains the dummy entry the\n\t// first log entry is not available).\n\tFirstIndex() (uint64, error)\n\t// Snapshot returns the most recent snapshot.\n\t// If snapshot is temporarily unavailable, it should return ErrSnapshotTemporarilyUnavailable,\n\t// so raft state machine could know that Storage needs some time to prepare\n\t// snapshot and call Snapshot later.\n\tSnapshot() (pb.Snapshot, error)\n}\n\ntype inMemStorageCallStats struct {\n\tinitialState, firstIndex, lastIndex, entries, term, snapshot int\n}\n\n// MemoryStorage implements the Storage interface backed by an\n// in-memory array.\ntype MemoryStorage struct {\n\t// Protects access to all fields. Most methods of MemoryStorage are\n\t// run on the raft goroutine, but Append() is run on an application\n\t// goroutine.\n\tsync.Mutex\n\n\thardState pb.HardState\n\tsnapshot  pb.Snapshot\n\t// ents[i] has raft log position i+snapshot.Metadata.Index\n\tents []pb.Entry\n\n\tcallStats inMemStorageCallStats\n}\n\n// NewMemoryStorage creates an empty MemoryStorage.\nfunc NewMemoryStorage() *MemoryStorage {\n\treturn &MemoryStorage{\n\t\t// When starting from scratch populate the list with a dummy entry at term zero.\n\t\tents: make([]pb.Entry, 1),\n\t}\n}\n\n// InitialState implements the Storage interface.\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) InitialState() (pb.HardState, pb.ConfState, error) {\n\tms.callStats.initialState++\n\treturn ms.hardState, ms.snapshot.Metadata.ConfState, nil\n}\n\n// SetHardState saves the current HardState.\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) SetHardState(st pb.HardState) error {\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\tms.hardState = st\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// Entries implements the Storage interface.\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) Entries(lo, hi, maxSize uint64) ([]pb.Entry, error) {\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\tms.callStats.entries++\n\toffset := ms.ents[0].Index\n\tif lo <= offset {\n\t\treturn nil, ErrCompacted\n\t}\n\tif hi > ms.lastIndex()+1 {\n\t\tgetLogger().Panicf(\"entries' hi(%d) is out of bound lastindex(%d)\", hi, ms.lastIndex())\n\t}\n\t// only contains dummy entries.\n\tif len(ms.ents) == 1 {\n\t\treturn nil, ErrUnavailable\n\t}\n","numLines":150,"startLine":1,"totalLines":314}}}

251→// Compact discards all log entries prior to compactIndex.\n   252→// It is the application's responsibility to not attempt to compact an index\n   253→// greater than raftLog.applied.\n   254→func (ms *MemoryStorage) Compact(compactIndex uint64) error {\n   255→\tms.Lock()\n   256→\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n   257→\toffset := ms.ents[0].Index\n   258→\tif compactIndex <= offset {\n 263→\t}\n   264→\n   265→\ti := compactIndex - offset\n   266→\t// NB: allocate a new slice instead of reusing the old ms.ents. Entries in\n   267→\t// ms.ents are immutable, and can be referenced from outside MemoryStorage\n   268→\t// through slices returned by ms.Entries().\n   269→\tents := make([]pb.Entry, 1, uint64(len(ms.ents))-i)\n   270→\tents[0].Index = ms.ents[i].Index\n   271→\tents[0].Term = ms.ents[i].Term\n   272→\tents = append(ents, ms.ents[i+1:]...)\n   273→\tms.ents = ents\n   274→\treturn nil\n   275→}\n   276→\n   277→// Append the new entries to storage.\n   278→// TODO (xiangli): ensure the entries are continuous and\n   279→// entries[0].Index > ms.entries[0].Index\n   280→func (ms *MemoryStorage) Append(entries []pb.Entry) error {\n   281→\tif len(entries) == 0 {\n   282→\t\treturn nil\n   283→\t}\n   284→\n   285→\tms.Lock()\n   286→\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n   287→\n   288→\tfirst := ms.firstIndex()\n   289→\tlast := entries[0].Index + uint64(len(entries)) - 1\n   290→\n   291→\t// shortcut if there is no new entry.\n   292→\tif last < first {\n   293→\t\treturn nil\n   294→\t}\n   295→\t// truncate compacted entries\n   296→\tif first > entries[0].Index {\n   297→\t\tentries = entries[first-entries[0].Index:]\n   298→\t}\n   299→\n   300→\toffset := entries[0].Index - ms.ents[0].Index\n   301→\tswitch {\n   302→\tcase uint64(len(ms.ents)) > offset:\n   303→\t\t// NB: full slice expression protects ms.ents at index >= offset from\n   304→\t\t// rewrites, as they may still be referenced from outside MemoryStorage.\n   305→\t\tms.ents = append(ms.ents[:offset:offset], entries...)\n   306→\tcase uint64(len(ms.ents)) == offset:\n   307→\t\tms.ents = append(ms.ents, entries...)\n   308→\tdefault:\n   309→\t\tgetLogger().Panicf(\"missing log entry [last: %d, append at: %d]\",\n   310→\t\t\tms.lastIndex(), entries[0].Index)\n   311→\t}\n   312→\treturn nil\n   313→}\n   314→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. interface when the requested log entries\n// are unavailable.\nvar ErrUnavailable = errors.New(\"requested entry at index is unavailable\")\n\n// ErrSnapshotTemporarilyUnavailable is returned by the Storage interface when the required\n// snapshot is temporarily unavailable.\nvar ErrSnapshotTemporarilyUnavailable = errors.New(\"snapshot is temporarily unavailable\")\n\n// Storage is an interface that may be implemented by the application\n// to retrieve log entries from storage.\n//\n// If any Storage method returns an error, the raft instance will\n// become inoperable and refuse to participate in elections; the\n// application is responsible for cleanup and recovery in this case.\ntype Storage interface {\n\t// TODO(tbg): split this into two interfaces, LogStorage and StateStorage.\n\n\t// InitialState returns the saved HardState and ConfState information.\n\tInitialState() (pb.HardState, pb.ConfState, error)\n\n\t// Entries returns a slice of consecutive log entries in the range [lo, hi),\n\t// starting from lo. The maxSize limits the total size of the log entries\n\t// returned, but Entries returns at least one entry if any.\n\t//\n\t// The caller of Entries owns the returned slice, and may append to it. The\n\t// individual entries in the slice must not be mutated, neither by the Storage\n\t// implementation nor the caller. Note that raft may forward these entries\n\t// back to the application via Ready struct, so the corresponding handler must\n\t// not mutate entries either (see comments in Ready struct).\n\t//\n\t// Since the caller may append to the returned slice, Storage implementation\n\t// must protect its state from corruption that such appends may cause. For\n\t// example, common ways to do so are:\n\t//  - allocate the slice before returning it (safest option),\n\t//  - return a slice protected by Go full slice expression, which causes\n\t//  copying on appends (see MemoryStorage).\n\t//\n\t// Returns ErrCompacted if entry lo has been compacted, or ErrUnavailable if\n\t// encountered an unavailable entry in [lo, hi).\n\tEntries(lo, hi, maxSize uint64) ([]pb.Entry, error)\n\n\t// Term returns the term of entry i, which must be in the range\n\t// [FirstIndex()-1, LastIndex()]. The term of the entry before\n\t// FirstIndex is retained for matching purposes even though the\n\t// rest of that entry may not be available.\n\tTerm(i uint64) (uint64, error)\n\t// LastIndex returns the index of the last entry in the log.\n\tLastIndex() (uint64, error)\n\t// FirstIndex returns the index of the first log entry that is\n\t// possibly available via Entries (older entries have been incorporated\n\t// into the latest Snapshot; if storage only contains the dummy entry the\n\t// first log entry is not available).\n\tFirstIndex() (uint64, error)\n\t// Snapshot returns the most recent snapshot.\n\t// If snapshot is temporarily unavailable, it should return ErrSnapshotTemporarilyUnavailable,\n\t// so raft state machine could know that Storage needs some time to prepare\n\t// snapshot and call Snapshot later.\n\tSnapshot() (pb.Snapshot, error)\n}\n\ntype inMemStorageCallStats struct {\n\tinitialState, firstIndex, lastIndex, entries, term, snapshot int\n}\n\n// MemoryStorage implements the Storage interface backed by an\n// in-memory array.\ntype MemoryStorage struct {\n\t// Protects access to all fields. Most methods of MemoryStorage are\n\t// run on the raft goroutine, but Append() is run on an application\n\t// goroutine.\n\tsync.Mutex\n\n\thardState pb.HardState\n\tsnapshot  pb.Snapshot\n\t// ents[i] has raft log position i+snapshot.Metadata.Index\n\tents []pb.Entry\n\n\tcallStats inMemStorageCallStats\n}\n\n// NewMemoryStorage creates an empty MemoryStorage.\nfunc NewMemoryStorage() *MemoryStorage {\n\treturn &MemoryStorage{\n\t\t// When starting from scratch populate the list with a dummy entry at term zero.\n\t\tents: make([]pb.Entry, 1),\n\t}\n}\n\n// InitialState implements the Storage interface.\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) InitialState() (pb.HardState, pb.ConfState, error) {\n\tms.callStats.initialState++\n\treturn ms.hardState, ms.snapshot.Metadata.ConfState, nil\n}\n\n// SetHardState saves the current HardState.\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) SetHardState(st pb.HardState) error {\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\tms.hardState = st\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// Entries implements the Storage interface.\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) Entries(lo, hi, maxSize uint64) ([]pb.Entry, error) {\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\tms.callStats.entries++\n\toffset := ms.ents[0].Index\n\tif lo <= offset {\n\t\treturn nil, ErrCompacted\n\t}\n\tif hi > ms.lastIndex()+1 {\n\t\tgetLogger().Panicf(\"entries' hi(%d) is out of bound lastindex(%d)\", hi, ms.lastIndex())\n\t}\n\t// only contains dummy entries.\n\tif len(ms.ents) == 1 {\n\t\treturn nil, ErrUnavailable\n\t}\n\n\tents := limitSize(ms.ents[lo-offset:hi-offset], entryEncodingSize(maxSize))\n\t// NB: use the full slice expression to limit what the caller can do with the\n\t// returned slice. For example, an append will reallocate and copy this slice\n\t// instead of corrupting the neighbouring ms.ents.\n\treturn ents[:len(ents):len(ents)], nil\n}\n\n// Term implements the Storage interface.\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) Term(i uint64) (uint64, error) {\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\tms.callStats.term++\n\toffset := ms.ents[0].Index\n\tif i < offset {\n\t\treturn 0, ErrCompacted\n\t}\n\tif int(i-offset) >= len(ms.ents) {\n\t\treturn 0, ErrUnavailable\n\t}\n\treturn ms.ents[i-offset].Term, nil\n}\n\n// LastIndex implements the Storage interface.\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) LastIndex() (uint64, error) {\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\tms.callStats.lastIndex++\n\treturn ms.lastIndex(), nil\n}\n\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) lastIndex() uint64 {\n\treturn ms.ents[0].Index + uint64(len(ms.ents)) - 1\n}\n\n// FirstIndex implements the Storage interface.\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) FirstIndex() (uint64, error) {\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\tms.callStats.firstIndex++\n\treturn ms.firstIndex(), nil\n}\n\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) firstIndex() uint64 {\n\treturn ms.ents[0].Index + 1\n}\n\n// Snapshot implements the Storage interface.\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) Snapshot() (pb.Snapshot, error) {\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\tms.callStats.snapshot++\n\treturn ms.snapshot, nil\n}\n\n// ApplySnapshot overwrites the contents of this Storage object with\n// those of the given snapshot.\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) ApplySnapshot(snap pb.Snapshot) error {\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\n\t//handle check for old snapshot being applied\n\tmsIndex := ms.snapshot.Metadata.Index\n\tsnapIndex := snap.Metadata.Index\n\t// During bootstrap, applications (e.g., etcd) may initialize only the\n\t// ConfState in the snapshot. In this case, both the snapshot index and\n\t// term are 0.\n\tif msIndex != 0 && msIndex >= snapIndex {\n\t\treturn ErrSnapOutOfDate\n\t}\n\n\tms.snapshot = snap\n\tms.ents = []pb.Entry{{Term: snap.Metadata.Term, Index: snap.Metadata.Index}}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// CreateSnapshot makes a snapshot which can be retrieved with Snapshot() and\n// can be used to reconstruct the state at that point.\n// If any configuration changes have been made since the last compaction,\n// the result of the last ApplyConfChange must be passed in.\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) CreateSnapshot(i uint64, cs *pb.ConfState, data []byte) (pb.Snapshot, error) {\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\tif i <= ms.snapshot.Metadata.Index {\n\t\treturn pb.Snapshot{}, ErrSnapOutOfDate\n\t}\n\n\toffset := ms.ents[0].Index\n\tif i > ms.lastIndex() {\n\t\tgetLogger().Panicf(\"snapshot %d is out of bound lastindex(%d)\", i, ms.lastIndex())\n\t}\n\n\tms.snapshot.Metadata.Index = i\n\tms.snapshot.Metadata.Term = ms.ents[i-offset].Term\n\tif cs != nil {\n\t\tms.snapshot.Metadata.ConfState = *cs\n\t}\n\tms.snapshot.Data = data\n\treturn ms.snapshot, nil\n}\n\n// Compact discards all log entries prior to compactIndex.\n// It is the application's responsibility to not attempt to compact an index\n// greater than raftLog.applied.\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) Compact(compactIndex uint64) error {\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\toffset := ms.ents[0].Index\n\tif compactIndex <= offset {\n\t\treturn ErrCompacted\n\t}\n\tif compactIndex > ms.lastIndex() {\n\t\tgetLogger().Panicf(\"compact %d is out of bound lastindex(%d)\", compactIndex, ms.lastIndex())\n\t}\n\n\ti := compactIndex - offset\n\t// NB: allocate a new slice instead of reusing the old ms.ents. Entries in\n\t// ms.ents are immutable, and can be referenced from outside MemoryStorage\n\t// through slices returned by ms.Entries().\n\tents := make([]pb.Entry, 1, uint64(len(ms.ents))-i)\n\tents[0].Index = ms.ents[i].Index\n\tents[0].Term = ms.ents[i].Term\n\tents = append(ents, ms.ents[i+1:]...)\n\tms.ents = ents\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// Append the new entries to storage.\n// TODO (xiangli): ensure the entries are continuous and\n// entries[0].Index > ms.entries[0].Index\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) Append(entries []pb.Entry) error {\n\tif len(entries) == 0 {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\n\tfirst := ms.firstIndex()\n\tlast := entries[0].Index + uint64(len(entries)) - 1\n\n\t// shortcut if there is no new entry.\n\tif last < first {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\t// truncate compacted entries\n\tif first > entries[0].Index {\n\t\tentries = entries[first-entries[0].Index:]\n\t}\n\n\toffset := entries[0].Index - ms.ents[0].Index\n\tswitch {\n\tcase uint64(len(ms.ents)) > offset:\n\t\t// NB: full slice expression protects ms.ents at index >= offset from\n\t\t// rewrites, as they may still be referenced from outside MemoryStorage.\n\t\tms.ents = append(ms.ents[:offset:offset], entries...)\n\tcase uint64(len(ms.ents)) == offset:\n\t\tms.ents = append(ms.ents, entries...)\n\tdefault:\n\t\tgetLogger().Panicf(\"missing log entry [last: %d, append at: %d]\",\n\t\t\tms.lastIndex(), entries[0].Index)\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n","numLines":314,"startLine":1,"totalLines":314}}}

 - allocate the slice before returning it (safest option),\n\t//  - return a slice protected by Go full slice expression, which causes\n\t//  copying on appends (see MemoryStorage).","newString":"//  - allocate the slice before returning it (safest option),\n\t//  - return a slice protected by Go full slice expression, which causes\n\t//  copying on appends (see InMemoryRaftLog).","originalFile":"// Copyright 2015 The etcd Authors\n//\n// Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n// you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n// You may obtain a copy of the License at\n//\n//     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n//\n// Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n// distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n// WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n// See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n// limitations under the License.\n\npackage raft\n\nimport (\n\t\"errors\"\n\t\"sync\"\n\n\tpb \"go.etcd.io/raft/v3/raftpb\"\n)\n\n// ErrCompacted is returned by Storage.Entries/Compact when a requested\n// index is unavailable because it predates the last snapshot.\nvar ErrCompacted = errors.New(\"requested index is unavailable due to compaction\")\n\n// ErrSnapOutOfDate is returned by Storage.CreateSnapshot when a requested\n// index is older than the existing snapshot.\nvar ErrSnapOutOfDate = errors.New(\"requested index is older than the existing snapshot\")\n\n// ErrUnavailable is returned by Storage interface when the requested log entries\n// are unavailable.\nvar ErrUnavailable = errors.New(\"requested entry at index is unavailable\")\n\n// ErrSnapshotTemporarilyUnavailable is returned by the Storage interface when the required\n// snapshot is temporarily unavailable.\nvar ErrSnapshotTemporarilyUnavailable = errors.New(\"snapshot is temporarily unavailable\")\n\n// Storage is an interface that may be implemented by the application\n// to retrieve log entries from storage.\n//\n// If any Storage method returns an error, the raft instance will\n// become inoperable and refuse to participate in elections; the\n// application is responsible for cleanup and recovery in this case.\ntype Storage interface {\n\t// TODO(tbg): split this into two interfaces, LogStorage and StateStorage.\n\n\t// InitialState returns the saved HardState and ConfState information.\n\tInitialState() (pb.HardState, pb.ConfState, error)\n\n\t// Entries returns a slice of consecutive log entries in the range [lo, hi),\n\t// starting from lo. The maxSize limits the total size of the log entries\n\t// returned, but Entries returns at least one entry if any.\n\t//\n\t// The caller of Entries owns the returned slice, and may append to it. The\n\t// individual entries in the slice must not be mutated, neither by the Storage\n\t// implementation nor the caller. Note that raft may forward these entries\n\t// back to the application via Ready struct, so the corresponding handler must\n\t// not mutate entries either (see comments in Ready struct).\n\t//\n\t// Since the caller may append to the returned slice, Storage implementation\n\t// must protect its state from corruption that such appends may cause. For\n\t// example, common ways to do so are:\n\t//  - allocate the slice before returning it (safest option),\n\t//  - return a slice protected by Go full slice expression, which causes\n\t//  copying on appends (see MemoryStorage).\n\t//\n\t// Returns ErrCompacted if entry lo has been compacted, or ErrUnavailable if\n\t// encountered an unavailable entry in [lo, hi).\n\tEntries(lo, hi, maxSize uint64) ([]pb.Entry, error)\n\n\t// Term returns the term of entry i, which must be in the range\n\t// [FirstIndex()-1, LastIndex()]. The term of the entry before\n\t// FirstIndex is retained for matching purposes even though the\n\t// rest of that entry may not be available.\n\tTerm(i uint64) (uint64, error)\n\t// LastIndex returns the index of the last entry in the log.\n\tLastIndex() (uint64, error)\n\t// FirstIndex returns the index of the first log entry that is\n\t// possibly available via Entries (older entries have been incorporated\n\t// into the latest Snapshot; if storage only contains the dummy entry the\n\t// first log entry is not available).\n\tFirstIndex() (uint64, error)\n\t// Snapshot returns the most recent snapshot.\n\t// If snapshot is temporarily unavailable, it should return ErrSnapshotTemporarilyUnavailable,\n\t// so raft state machine could know that Storage needs some time to prepare\n\t// snapshot and call Snapshot later.\n\tSnapshot() (pb.Snapshot, error)\n}\n\ntype inMemStorageCallStats struct {\n\tinitialState, firstIndex, lastIndex, entries, term, snapshot int\n}\n\n// MemoryStorage implements the Storage interface backed by an\n// in-memory array.\ntype MemoryStorage struct {\n\t// Protects access to all fields. Most methods of MemoryStorage are\n\t// run on the raft goroutine, but Append() is run on an application\n\t// goroutine.\n\tsync.Mutex\n\n\thardState pb.HardState\n\tsnapshot  pb.Snapshot\n\t// ents[i] has raft log position i+snapshot.Metadata.Index\n\tents []pb.Entry\n\n\tcallStats inMemStorageCallStats\n}\n\n// NewMemoryStorage creates an empty MemoryStorage.\nfunc NewMemoryStorage() *MemoryStorage {\n\treturn &MemoryStorage{\n\t\t// When starting from scratch populate the list with a dummy entry at term zero.\n\t\tents: make([]pb.Entry, 1),\n\t}\n}\n\n// InitialState implements the Storage interface.\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) InitialState() (pb.HardState, pb.ConfState, error) {\n\tms.callStats.initialState++\n\treturn ms.hardState, ms.snapshot.Metadata.ConfState, nil\n}\n\n// SetHardState saves the current HardState.\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) SetHardState(st pb.HardState) error {\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\tms.hardState = st\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// Entries implements the Storage interface.\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) Entries(lo, hi, maxSize uint64) ([]pb.Entry, error) {\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\tms.callStats.entries++\n\toffset := ms.ents[0].Index\n\tif lo <= offset {\n\t\treturn nil, ErrCompacted\n\t}\n\tif hi > ms.lastIndex()+1 {\n\t\tgetLogger().Panicf(\"entries' hi(%d) is out of bound lastindex(%d)\", hi, ms.lastIndex())\n\t}\n\t// only contains dummy entries.\n\tif len(ms.ents) == 1 {\n\t\treturn nil, ErrUnavailable\n\t}\n\n\tents := limitSize(ms.ents[lo-offset:hi-offset], entryEncodingSize(maxSize))\n\t// NB: use the full slice expression to limit what the caller can do with the\n\t// returned slice. For example, an append will reallocate and copy this slice\n\t// instead of corrupting the neighbouring ms.ents.\n\treturn ents[:len(ents):len(ents)], nil\n}\n\n// Term implements the Storage interface.\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) Term(i uint64) (uint64, error) {\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\tms.callStats.term++\n\toffset := ms.ents[0].Index\n\tif i < offset {\n\t\treturn 0, ErrCompacted\n\t}\n\tif int(i-offset) >= len(ms.ents) {\n\t\treturn 0, ErrUnavailable\n\t}\n\treturn ms.ents[i-offset].Term, nil\n}\n\n// LastIndex implements the Storage interface.\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) LastIndex() (uint64, error) {\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\tms.callStats.lastIndex++\n\treturn ms.lastIndex(), nil\n}\n\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) lastIndex() uint64 {\n\treturn ms.ents[0].Index + uint64(len(ms.ents)) - 1\n}\n\n// FirstIndex implements the Storage interface.\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) FirstIndex() (uint64, error) {\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\tms.callStats.firstIndex++\n\treturn ms.firstIndex(), nil\n}\n\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) firstIndex() uint64 {\n\treturn ms.ents[0].Index + 1\n}\n\n// Snapshot implements the Storage interface.\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) Snapshot() (pb.Snapshot, error) {\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\tms.callStats.snapshot++\n\treturn ms.snapshot, nil\n}\n\n// ApplySnapshot overwrites the contents of this Storage object with\n// those of the given snapshot.\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) ApplySnapshot(snap pb.Snapshot) error {\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\n\t//handle check for old snapshot being applied\n\tmsIndex := ms.snapshot.Metadata.Index\n\tsnapIndex := snap.Metadata.Index\n\t// During bootstrap, applications (e.g., etcd) may initialize only the\n\t// ConfState in the snapshot. implemented by the application\n// to retrieve log entries from storage.\n//\n// If any Storage method returns an error, the raft instance will\n// become inoperable and refuse to participate in elections; the\n// application is responsible for cleanup and recovery in this case.\ntype Storage interface {\n\t// TODO(tbg): split this into two interfaces, LogStorage and StateStorage.\n\n\t// InitialState returns the saved HardState and ConfState information.\n\tInitialState() (pb.HardState, pb.ConfState, error)\n\n\t// Entries returns a slice of consecutive log entries in the range [lo, hi),\n\t// starting from lo. The maxSize limits the total size of the log entries\n\t// returned, but Entries returns at least one entry if any.\n\t//\n\t// The caller of Entries owns the returned slice, and may append to it. The\n\t// individual entries in the slice must not be mutated, neither by the Storage\n\t// implementation nor the caller. Note that raft may forward these entries\n\t// back to the application via Ready struct, so the corresponding handler must\n\t// not mutate entries either (see comments in Ready struct).\n\t//\n\t// Since the caller may append to the returned slice, Storage implementation\n\t// must protect its state from corruption that such appends may cause. For\n\t// example, common ways to do so are:\n\t//  - allocate the slice before returning it (safest option),\n\t//  - return a slice protected by Go full slice expression, which causes\n\t//  copying on appends (see InMemoryRaftLog).\n\t//\n\t// Returns ErrCompacted if entry lo has been compacted, or ErrUnavailable if\n\t// encountered an unavailable entry in [lo, hi).\n\tEntries(lo, hi, maxSize uint64) ([]pb.Entry, error)\n\n\t// Term returns the term of entry i, which must be in the range\n\t// [FirstIndex()-1, LastIndex()]. raft\n\nimport (\n\t\"errors\"\n\t\"sync\"\n\n\tpb \"go.etcd.io/raft/v3/raftpb\"\n)\n\n// ErrCompacted is returned by Storage.Entries/Compact when a requested\n// index is unavailable because it predates the last snapshot.\nvar ErrCompacted = errors.New(\"requested index is unavailable due to compaction\")\n\n// ErrSnapOutOfDate is returned by Storage.CreateSnapshot when a requested\n// index is older than the existing snapshot.\nvar ErrSnapOutOfDate = errors.New(\"requested index is older than the existing snapshot\")\n\n// ErrUnavailable is returned by Storage interface when the requested log entries\n// are unavailable.\nvar ErrUnavailable = errors.New(\"requested entry at index is unavailable\")\n\n// ErrSnapshotTemporarilyUnavailable is returned by the Storage interface when the required\n// snapshot is temporarily unavailable.\nvar ErrSnapshotTemporarilyUnavailable = errors.New(\"snapshot is temporarily unavailable\")\n\n// Storage is an interface that may be implemented by the application\n// to retrieve log entries from storage.\n//\n// If any Storage method returns an error, the raft instance will\n// become inoperable and refuse to participate in elections; the\n// application is responsible for cleanup and recovery in this case.\ntype Storage interface {\n\t// TODO(tbg): split this into two interfaces, LogStorage and StateStorage.\n\n\t// InitialState returns the saved HardState and ConfState information.\n\tInitialState() (pb.HardState, pb.ConfState, error)\n\n\t// Entries returns a slice of consecutive log entries in the range [lo, hi),\n\t// starting from lo. The maxSize limits the total size of the log entries\n\t// returned, but Entries returns at least one entry if any.\n\t//\n\t// The caller of Entries owns the returned slice, and may append to it. The\n\t// individual entries in the slice must not be mutated, neither by the Storage\n\t// implementation nor the caller. Note that raft may forward these entries\n\t// back to the application via Ready struct, so the corresponding handler must\n\t// not mutate entries either (see comments in Ready struct).\n\t//\n\t// Since the caller may append to the returned slice, Storage implementation\n\t// must protect its state from corruption that such appends may cause. For\n\t// example, common ways to do so are:\n\t//  - allocate the slice before returning it (safest option),\n\t//  - return a slice protected by Go full slice expression, which causes\n\t//  copying on appends (see InMemoryRaftLog).\n\t//\n\t// Returns ErrCompacted if entry lo has been compacted, or ErrUnavailable if\n\t// encountered an unavailable entry in [lo, hi).\n\tEntries(lo, hi, maxSize uint64) ([]pb.Entry, error)\n\n\t// Term returns the term of entry i, which must be in the range\n\t// [FirstIndex()-1, LastIndex()]. The term of the entry before\n\t// FirstIndex is retained for matching purposes even though the\n\t// rest of that entry may not be available.\n\tTerm(i uint64) (uint64, error)\n\t// LastIndex returns the index of the last entry in the log.\n\tLastIndex() (uint64, error)\n\t// FirstIndex returns the index of the first log entry that is\n\t// possibly available via Entries (older entries have been incorporated\n\t// into the latest Snapshot; if storage only contains the dummy entry the\n\t// first log entry is not available).\n\tFirstIndex() (uint64, error)\n\t// Snapshot returns the most recent snapshot.\n\t// If snapshot is temporarily unavailable, it should return ErrSnapshotTemporarilyUnavailable,\n\t// so raft state machine could know that Storage needs some time to prepare\n\t// snapshot and call Snapshot later.\n\tSnapshot() (pb.Snapshot, error)\n}\n\ntype inMemStorageCallStats struct {\n\tinitialState, firstIndex, lastIndex, entries, term, snapshot int\n}\n\n// InMemoryRaftLog implements the Storage interface backed by an\n// in-memory array.\ntype InMemoryRaftLog struct {\n\t// Protects access to all fields. Most methods of InMemoryRaftLog are\n\t// run on the raft goroutine, but Append() is run on an application\n\t// goroutine.\n\tsync.Mutex\n\n\thardState pb.HardState\n\tsnapshot  pb.Snapshot\n\t// ents[i] has raft log position i+snapshot.Metadata.Index\n\tents []pb.Entry\n\n\tcallStats inMemStorageCallStats\n}\n\n// NewMemoryStorage creates an empty MemoryStorage.\nfunc NewMemoryStorage() *MemoryStorage {\n\treturn &MemoryStorage{\n\t\t// When starting from scratch populate the list with a dummy entry at term zero.\n\t\tents: make([]pb.Entry, 1),\n\t}\n}\n\n// InitialState implements the Storage interface.\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) InitialState() (pb.HardState, pb.ConfState, error) {\n\tms.callStats.initialState++\n\treturn ms.hardState, ms.snapshot.Metadata.ConfState, nil\n}\n\n// SetHardState saves the current HardState.\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) SetHardState(st pb.HardState) error {\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\tms.hardState = st\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// Entries implements the Storage interface.\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) Entries(lo, hi, maxSize uint64) ([]pb.Entry, error) {\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\tms.callStats.entries++\n\toffset := ms.ents[0].Index\n\tif lo <= offset {\n\t\treturn nil, ErrCompacted\n\t}\n\tif hi > ms.lastIndex()+1 {\n\t\tgetLogger().Panicf(\"entries' hi(%d) is out of bound lastindex(%d)\", hi, ms.lastIndex())\n\t}\n\t// only contains dummy entries.\n\tif len(ms.ents) == 1 {\n\t\treturn nil, ErrUnavailable\n\t}\n\n\tents := limitSize(ms.ents[lo-offset:hi-offset], entryEncodingSize(maxSize))\n\t// NB: use the full slice expression to limit what the caller can do with the\n\t// returned slice. For example, an append will reallocate and copy this slice\n\t// instead of corrupting the neighbouring ms.ents.\n\treturn ents[:len(ents):len(ents)], nil\n}\n\n// Term implements the Storage interface.\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) Term(i uint64) (uint64, error) {\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\tms.callStats.term++\n\toffset := ms.ents[0].Index\n\tif i < offset {\n\t\treturn 0, ErrCompacted\n\t}\n\tif int(i-offset) >= len(ms.ents) {\n\t\treturn 0, ErrUnavailable\n\t}\n\treturn ms.ents[i-offset].Term, nil\n}\n\n// LastIndex implements the Storage interface.\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) LastIndex() (uint64, error) {\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\tms.callStats.lastIndex++\n\treturn ms.lastIndex(), nil\n}\n\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) lastIndex() uint64 {\n\treturn ms.ents[0].Index + uint64(len(ms.ents)) - 1\n}\n\n// FirstIndex implements the Storage interface.\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) FirstIndex() (uint64, error) {\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\tms.callStats.firstIndex++\n\treturn ms.firstIndex(), nil\n}\n\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) firstIndex() uint64 {\n\treturn ms.ents[0].Index + 1\n}\n\n// Snapshot implements the Storage interface.\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) Snapshot() (pb.Snapshot, error) {\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\tms.callStats.snapshot++\n\treturn ms.snapshot, nil\n}\n\n// ApplySnapshot overwrites the contents of this Storage object with\n// those of the given snapshot.\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) ApplySnapshot(snap pb.Snapshot) error {\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\n\t//handle check for old snapshot being applied\n\tmsIndex := ms.snapshot.Metadata.Index\n\tsnapIndex := snap.Metadata.Index\n\t// During bootstrap, applications (e.g., etcd) may initialize only the\n\t// ConfState in the snapshot. In this case, both the snapshot index and\n\t// term are 0.\n\tif msIndex != 0 && msIndex >= snapIndex {\n\t\treturn ErrSnapOutOfDate\n\t}\n\n\tms.snapshot = snap\n\tms.ents = []pb.Entry{{Term: snap.Metadata.Term, Index: snap.Metadata.Index}}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// CreateSnapshot makes a snapshot which can be retrieved with Snapshot() and\n// can be used to reconstruct the state at that point.\n// If any configuration changes have been made since the last compaction,\n// the result of the last ApplyConfChange must be passed in.\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) CreateSnapshot(i uint64, cs *pb.ConfState, data []byte) (pb.Snapshot, error) {\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\tif i <= ms.snapshot.Metadata.Index {\n\t\treturn pb.Snapshot{}, ErrSnapOutOfDate\n\t}\n\n\toffset := ms.ents[0].Index\n\tif i > ms.lastIndex() {\n\t\tgetLogger().Panicf(\"snapshot %d is out of bound lastindex(%d)\", i, ms.lastIndex())\n\t}\n\n\tms.snapshot.Metadata.Index = i\n\tms.snapshot.Metadata.Term = ms.ents[i-offset].Term\n\tif cs != nil {\n\t\tms.snapshot.Metadata.ConfState = *cs\n\t}\n\tms.snapshot.Data = data\n\treturn ms.snapshot, nil\n}\n\n// Compact discards all log entries prior to compactIndex.\n// It is the application's responsibility to not attempt to compact an index\n// greater than raftLog.applied.\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) Compact(compactIndex uint64) error {\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\toffset := ms.ents[0].Index\n\tif compactIndex <= offset {\n\t\treturn ErrCompacted\n\t}\n\tif compactIndex > ms.lastIndex() {\n\t\tgetLogger().Panicf(\"compact %d is out of bound lastindex(%d)\", compactIndex, ms.lastIndex())\n\t}\n\n\ti := compactIndex - offset\n\t// NB: allocate a new slice instead of reusing the old ms.ents. Entries in\n\t// ms.ents are immutable, and can be referenced from outside MemoryStorage\n\t// through slices returned by ms.Entries().\n\tents := make([]pb.Entry, 1, uint64(len(ms.ents))-i)\n\tents[0].Index = ms.ents[i].Index\n\tents[0].Term = ms.ents[i].Term\n\tents = append(ents, ms.ents[i+1:]...)\n\tms.ents = ents\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// Append the new entries to storage.\n// TODO (xiangli): ensure the entries are continuous and\n// entries[0].Index > ms.entries[0].Index\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) Append(entries []pb.Entry) error {\n\tif len(entries) == 0 {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\n\tfirst := ms.firstIndex()\n\tlast := entries[0].Index + uint64(len(entries)) - 1\n\n\t// shortcut if there is no new entry.\n\tif last < first {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\t// truncate compacted entries\n\tif first > entries[0].Index {\n\t\tentries = entries[first-entries[0].Index:]\n\t}\n\n\toffset := entries[0].Index - ms.ents[0].Index\n\tswitch {\n\tcase uint64(len(ms.ents)) > offset:\n\t\t// NB: full slice expression protects ms.ents at index >= offset from\n\t\t// rewrites, as they may still be referenced from outside MemoryStorage.\n\t\tms.ents = append(ms.ents[:offset:offset], entries...)\n\tcase uint64(len(ms.ents)) == offset:\n\t\tms.ents = append(ms.ents, entries...)\n\tdefault:\n\t\tgetLogger().Panicf(\"missing log entry [last: %d, append at: %d]\",\n\t\t\tms.lastIndex(), entries[0].Index)\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":109,"oldLines":9,"newStart":109,"newLines":9,"lines":["   callStats inMemStorageCallStats"," }"," ","-// NewMemoryStorage creates an empty MemoryStorage.","-func NewMemoryStorage() *MemoryStorage {","-  return &MemoryStorage{","+// NewInMemoryRaftLog creates an empty InMemoryRaftLog.","+func NewInMemoryRaftLog() *InMemoryRaftLog {","+  return &InMemoryRaftLog{","     // When starting from scratch populate the list with a dummy entry at term zero.","     ents: make([]pb.Entry, 1),","   }"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

participate in elections; the\n// application is responsible for cleanup and recovery in this case.\ntype Storage interface {\n\t// TODO(tbg): split this into two interfaces, LogStorage and the\n// application is responsible for cleanup and recovery in this case.\ntype Storage interface {\n\t// TODO(tbg): split this into two interfaces, LogStorage and StateStorage.\n\n\t// InitialState returns the saved HardState and ConfState information.\n\tInitialState() (pb.HardState, pb.ConfState, error)\n\n\t// Entries returns a slice of consecutive log entries in the range [lo, hi),\n\t// starting from lo. The maxSize limits the total size of the log entries\n\t// returned, but Entries returns at least one entry if any.\n\t//\n\t// The caller of Entries owns the returned slice, and may append to it. The\n\t// individual entries in the slice must not be mutated, neither by the Storage\n\t// implementation nor the caller. Note that raft may forward these entries\n\t// back to the application via Ready struct, so the corresponding handler must\n\t// not mutate entries either (see comments in Ready struct).\n\t//\n\t// Since the caller may append to the returned slice, Storage implementation\n\t// must protect its state from corruption that such appends may cause. For\n\t// example, common ways to do so are:\n\t//  - allocate the slice before returning it (safest option),\n\t//  - return a slice protected by Go full slice expression, which causes\n\t//  copying on appends (see InMemoryRaftLog).\n\t//\n\t// Returns ErrCompacted if entry lo has been compacted, or ErrUnavailable if\n\t// encountered an unavailable entry in [lo, hi).\n\tEntries(lo, hi, maxSize uint64) ([]pb.Entry, error)\n\n\t// Term returns the term of entry i, which must be in the range\n\t// [FirstIndex()-1, LastIndex()]. The term of the entry before\n\t// FirstIndex is retained for matching purposes even though the\n\t// rest of that entry may not be available.\n\tTerm(i uint64) (uint64, error)\n\t// LastIndex returns the index of the last entry in the log.\n\tLastIndex() (uint64, error)\n\t// FirstIndex returns the index of the first log entry that is\n\t// possibly available via Entries (older entries have been incorporated\n\t// into the latest Snapshot; if storage only contains the dummy entry the\n\t// first log entry is not available).\n\tFirstIndex() (uint64, error)\n\t// Snapshot returns the most recent snapshot.\n\t// If snapshot is temporarily unavailable, it should return ErrSnapshotTemporarilyUnavailable,\n\t// so raft state machine could know that Storage needs some time to prepare\n\t// snapshot and call Snapshot later.\n\tSnapshot() (pb.Snapshot, error)\n}\n\ntype inMemStorageCallStats struct {\n\tinitialState, firstIndex, lastIndex, entries, term, snapshot int\n}\n\n// InMemoryRaftLog implements the Storage interface backed by an\n// in-memory array.\ntype InMemoryRaftLog struct {\n\t// Protects access to all fields. Most methods of InMemoryRaftLog are\n\t// run on the raft goroutine, but Append() is run on an application\n\t// goroutine.\n\tsync.Mutex\n\n\thardState pb.HardState\n\tsnapshot  pb.Snapshot\n\t// ents[i] has raft log position i+snapshot.Metadata.Index\n\tents []pb.Entry\n\n\tcallStats inMemStorageCallStats\n}\n\n// NewInMemoryRaftLog creates an empty InMemoryRaftLog.\nfunc NewInMemoryRaftLog() *InMemoryRaftLog {\n\treturn &InMemoryRaftLog{\n\t\t// When starting from scratch populate the list with a dummy entry at term zero.\n\t\tents: make([]pb.Entry, 1),\n\t}\n}\n\n// InitialState implements the Storage interface.\nfunc (ms *InMemoryRaftLog) InitialState() (pb.HardState, pb.ConfState, error) {\n\tms.callStats.initialState++\n\treturn ms.hardState, ms.snapshot.Metadata.ConfState, nil\n}\n\n// SetHardState saves the current HardState.\nfunc (ms *InMemoryRaftLog) SetHardState(st pb.HardState) error {\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\tms.hardState = st\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// Entries implements the Storage interface.\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) Entries(lo, hi, maxSize uint64) ([]pb.Entry, error) {\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\tms.callStats.entries++\n\toffset := ms.ents[0].Index\n\tif lo <= offset {\n\t\treturn nil, ErrCompacted\n\t}\n\tif hi > ms.lastIndex()+1 {\n\t\tgetLogger().Panicf(\"entries' hi(%d) is out of bound lastindex(%d)\", hi, ms.lastIndex())\n\t}\n\t// only contains dummy entries.\n\tif len(ms.ents) == 1 {\n\t\treturn nil, ErrUnavailable\n\t}\n\n\tents := limitSize(ms.ents[lo-offset:hi-offset], entryEncodingSize(maxSize))\n\t// NB: use the full slice expression to limit what the caller can do with the\n\t// returned slice. For example, an append will reallocate and copy this slice\n\t// instead of corrupting the neighbouring ms.ents.\n\treturn ents[:len(ents):len(ents)], nil\n}\n\n// Term implements the Storage interface.\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) Term(i uint64) (uint64, error) {\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\tms.callStats.term++\n\toffset := ms.ents[0].Index\n\tif i < offset {\n\t\treturn 0, ErrCompacted\n\t}\n\tif int(i-offset) >= len(ms.ents) {\n\t\treturn 0, ErrUnavailable\n\t}\n\treturn ms.ents[i-offset].Term, nil\n}\n\n// LastIndex implements the Storage interface.\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) LastIndex() (uint64, error) {\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\tms.callStats.lastIndex++\n\treturn ms.lastIndex(), nil\n}\n\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) lastIndex() uint64 {\n\treturn ms.ents[0].Index + uint64(len(ms.ents)) - 1\n}\n\n// FirstIndex implements the Storage interface.\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) FirstIndex() (uint64, error) {\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\tms.callStats.firstIndex++\n\treturn ms.firstIndex(), nil\n}\n\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) firstIndex() uint64 {\n\treturn ms.ents[0].Index + 1\n}\n\n// Snapshot implements the Storage interface.\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) Snapshot() (pb.Snapshot, error) {\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\tms.callStats.snapshot++\n\treturn ms.snapshot, nil\n}\n\n// ApplySnapshot overwrites the contents of this Storage object with\n// those of the given snapshot.\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) ApplySnapshot(snap pb.Snapshot) error {\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\n\t//handle check for old snapshot being applied\n\tmsIndex := ms.snapshot.Metadata.Index\n\tsnapIndex := snap.Metadata.Index\n\t// During bootstrap, applications (e.g., etcd) may initialize only the\n\t// ConfState in the snapshot. In this case, both the snapshot index and\n\t// term are 0.\n\tif msIndex != 0 && msIndex >= snapIndex {\n\t\treturn ErrSnapOutOfDate\n\t}\n\n\tms.snapshot = snap\n\tms.ents = []pb.Entry{{Term: snap.Metadata.Term, Index: snap.Metadata.Index}}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// CreateSnapshot makes a snapshot which can be retrieved with Snapshot() and\n// can be used to reconstruct the state at that point.\n// If any configuration changes have been made since the last compaction,\n// the result of the last ApplyConfChange must be passed in.\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) CreateSnapshot(i uint64, cs *pb.ConfState, data []byte) (pb.Snapshot, error) {\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\tif i <= ms.snapshot.Metadata.Index {\n\t\treturn pb.Snapshot{}, ErrSnapOutOfDate\n\t}\n\n\toffset := ms.ents[0].Index\n\tif i > ms.lastIndex() {\n\t\tgetLogger().Panicf(\"snapshot %d is out of bound lastindex(%d)\", i, ms.lastIndex())\n\t}\n\n\tms.snapshot.Metadata.Index = i\n\tms.snapshot.Metadata.Term = ms.ents[i-offset].Term\n\tif cs != nil {\n\t\tms.snapshot.Metadata.ConfState = *cs\n\t}\n\tms.snapshot.Data = data\n\treturn ms.snapshot, nil\n}\n\n// Compact discards all log entries prior to compactIndex.\n// It is the application's responsibility to not attempt to compact an index\n// greater than raftLog.applied.\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) Compact(compactIndex uint64) error {\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\toffset := ms.ents[0].Index\n\tif compactIndex <= offset {\n\t\treturn ErrCompacted\n\t}\n\tif compactIndex > ms.lastIndex() {\n\t\tgetLogger().Panicf(\"compact %d is out of bound lastindex(%d)\", compactIndex, ms.lastIndex())\n\t}\n\n\ti := compactIndex - offset\n\t// NB: allocate a new slice instead of reusing the old ms.ents. Entries in\n\t// ms.ents are immutable, and can be referenced from outside MemoryStorage\n\t// through slices returned by ms.Entries().\n\tents := make([]pb.Entry, 1, uint64(len(ms.ents))-i)\n\tents[0].Index = ms.ents[i].Index\n\tents[0].Term = ms.ents[i].Term\n\tents = append(ents, ms.ents[i+1:]...)\n\tms.ents = ents\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// Append the new entries to storage.\n// TODO (xiangli): ensure the entries are continuous and\n// entries[0].Index > ms.entries[0].Index\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) Append(entries []pb.Entry) error {\n\tif len(entries) == 0 {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\n\tfirst := ms.firstIndex()\n\tlast := entries[0].Index + uint64(len(entries)) - 1\n\n\t// shortcut if there is no new entry.\n\tif last < first {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\t// truncate compacted entries\n\tif first > entries[0].Index {\n\t\tentries = entries[first-entries[0].Index:]\n\t}\n\n\toffset := entries[0].Index - ms.ents[0].Index\n\tswitch {\n\tcase uint64(len(ms.ents)) > offset:\n\t\t// NB: full slice expression protects ms.ents at index >= offset from\n\t\t// rewrites, as they may still be referenced from outside MemoryStorage.\n\t\tms.ents = append(ms.ents[:offset:offset], entries...)\n\tcase uint64(len(ms.ents)) == offset:\n\t\tms.ents = append(ms.ents, entries...)\n\tdefault:\n\t\tgetLogger().Panicf(\"missing log entry [last: %d, append at: %d]\",\n\t\t\tms.lastIndex(), entries[0].Index)\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":132,"oldLines":7,"newStart":132,"newLines":7,"lines":[" }"," "," // Entries implements the Storage interface.","-func (ms *MemoryStorage) Entries(lo, hi, maxSize uint64) ([]pb.Entry, error) {","+func (ms *InMemoryRaftLog) Entries(lo, hi, maxSize uint64) ([]pb.Entry, error) {","   ms.Lock()","   defer ms.Unlock()","   ms.callStats.entries++"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

raft\n\nimport (\n\t\"errors\"\n\t\"sync\"\n\n\tpb \"go.etcd.io/raft/v3/raftpb\"\n)\n\n// ErrCompacted is returned by Storage.Entries/Compact when a requested\n// index is unavailable because it predates the last snapshot.\nvar ErrCompacted = errors.New(\"requested index is unavailable due to compaction\")\n\n// ErrSnapOutOfDate is returned by Storage.CreateSnapshot when a requested\n// index is older than the existing snapshot.\nvar ErrSnapOutOfDate = errors.New(\"requested index is older than the existing snapshot\")\n\n// ErrUnavailable is returned by Storage interface when the requested log entries\n// are unavailable.\nvar ErrUnavailable = errors.New(\"requested entry at index is unavailable\")\n\n// ErrSnapshotTemporarilyUnavailable is returned by the Storage interface when the required\n// snapshot is temporarily unavailable.\nvar ErrSnapshotTemporarilyUnavailable = errors.New(\"snapshot is temporarily unavailable\")\n\n// Storage is an interface that may be implemented by the application\n// to retrieve log entries from storage.\n//\n// If any Storage method returns an error, the raft instance will\n// become inoperable and refuse to participate in elections; the\n// application is responsible for cleanup and recovery in this case.\ntype Storage interface {\n\t// TODO(tbg): split this into two interfaces, LogStorage and StateStorage.\n\n\t// InitialState returns the saved HardState and ConfState information.\n\tInitialState() (pb.HardState, pb.ConfState, error)\n\n\t// Entries returns a slice of consecutive log entries in the range [lo, hi),\n\t// starting from lo. The maxSize limits the total size of the log entries\n\t// returned, but Entries returns at least one entry if any.\n\t//\n\t// The caller of Entries owns the returned slice, and may append to it. The\n\t// individual entries in the slice must not be mutated, neither by the Storage\n\t// implementation nor the caller. Note that raft may forward these entries\n\t// back to the application via Ready struct, so the corresponding handler must\n\t// not mutate entries either (see comments in Ready struct).\n\t//\n\t// Since the caller may append to the returned slice, Storage implementation\n\t// must protect its state from corruption that such appends may cause. For\n\t// example, common ways to do so are:\n\t//  - allocate the slice before returning it (safest option),\n\t//  - return a slice protected by Go full slice expression, which causes\n\t//  copying on appends (see InMemoryRaftLog).\n\t//\n\t// Returns ErrCompacted if entry lo has been compacted, or ErrUnavailable if\n\t// encountered an unavailable entry in [lo, hi).\n\tEntries(lo, hi, maxSize uint64) ([]pb.Entry, error)\n\n\t// Term returns the term of entry i, which must be in the range\n\t// [FirstIndex()-1, LastIndex()]. The term of the entry before\n\t// FirstIndex is retained for matching purposes even though the\n\t// rest of that entry may not be available.\n\tTerm(i uint64) (uint64, error)\n\t// LastIndex returns the index of the last entry in the log.\n\tLastIndex() (uint64, error)\n\t// FirstIndex returns the index of the first log entry that is\n\t// possibly available via Entries (older entries have been incorporated\n\t// into the latest Snapshot; if storage only contains the dummy entry the\n\t// first log entry is not available).\n\tFirstIndex() (uint64, error)\n\t// Snapshot returns the most recent snapshot.\n\t// If snapshot is temporarily unavailable, it should return ErrSnapshotTemporarilyUnavailable,\n\t// so raft state machine could know that Storage needs some time to prepare\n\t// snapshot and call Snapshot later.\n\tSnapshot() (pb.Snapshot, error)\n}\n\ntype inMemStorageCallStats struct {\n\tinitialState, firstIndex, lastIndex, entries, term, snapshot int\n}\n\n// InMemoryRaftLog implements the Storage interface backed by an\n// in-memory array.\ntype InMemoryRaftLog struct {\n\t// Protects access to all fields. Most methods of InMemoryRaftLog are\n\t// run on the raft goroutine, but Append() is run on an application\n\t// goroutine.\n\tsync.Mutex\n\n\thardState pb.HardState\n\tsnapshot  pb.Snapshot\n\t// ents[i] has raft log position i+snapshot.Metadata.Index\n\tents []pb.Entry\n\n\tcallStats inMemStorageCallStats\n}\n\n// NewInMemoryRaftLog creates an empty InMemoryRaftLog.\nfunc NewInMemoryRaftLog() *InMemoryRaftLog {\n\treturn &InMemoryRaftLog{\n\t\t// When starting from scratch populate the list with a dummy entry at term zero.\n\t\tents: make([]pb.Entry, 1),\n\t}\n}\n\n// InitialState implements the Storage interface.\nfunc (ms *InMemoryRaftLog) InitialState() (pb.HardState, pb.ConfState, error) {\n\tms.callStats.initialState++\n\treturn ms.hardState, ms.snapshot.Metadata.ConfState, nil\n}\n\n// SetHardState saves the current HardState.\nfunc (ms *InMemoryRaftLog) SetHardState(st pb.HardState) error {\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\tms.hardState = st\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// Entries implements the Storage interface.\nfunc (ms *InMemoryRaftLog) Entries(lo, hi, maxSize uint64) ([]pb.Entry, error) {\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\tms.callStats.entries++\n\toffset := ms.ents[0].Index\n\tif lo <= offset {\n\t\treturn nil, ErrCompacted\n\t}\n\tif hi > ms.lastIndex()+1 {\n\t\tgetLogger().Panicf(\"entries' hi(%d) is out of bound lastindex(%d)\", hi, ms.lastIndex())\n\t}\n\t// only contains dummy entries.\n\tif len(ms.ents) == 1 {\n\t\treturn nil, ErrUnavailable\n\t}\n\n\tents := limitSize(ms.ents[lo-offset:hi-offset], entryEncodingSize(maxSize))\n\t// NB: use the full slice expression to limit what the caller can do with the\n\t// returned slice. For example, an append will reallocate and copy this slice\n\t// instead of corrupting the neighbouring ms.ents.\n\treturn ents[:len(ents):len(ents)], nil\n}\n\n// Term implements the Storage interface.\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) Term(i uint64) (uint64, error) {\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\tms.callStats.term++\n\toffset := ms.ents[0].Index\n\tif i < offset {\n\t\treturn 0, ErrCompacted\n\t}\n\tif int(i-offset) >= len(ms.ents) {\n\t\treturn 0, ErrUnavailable\n\t}\n\treturn ms.ents[i-offset].Term, nil\n}\n\n// LastIndex implements the Storage interface.\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) LastIndex() (uint64, error) {\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\tms.callStats.lastIndex++\n\treturn ms.lastIndex(), nil\n}\n\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) lastIndex() uint64 {\n\treturn ms.ents[0].Index + uint64(len(ms.ents)) - 1\n}\n\n// FirstIndex implements the Storage interface.\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) FirstIndex() (uint64, error) {\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\tms.callStats.firstIndex++\n\treturn ms.firstIndex(), nil\n}\n\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) firstIndex() uint64 {\n\treturn ms.ents[0].Index + 1\n}\n\n// Snapshot implements the Storage interface.\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) Snapshot() (pb.Snapshot, error) {\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\tms.callStats.snapshot++\n\treturn ms.snapshot, nil\n}\n\n// ApplySnapshot overwrites the contents of this Storage object with\n// those of the given snapshot.\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) ApplySnapshot(snap pb.Snapshot) error {\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\n\t//handle check for old snapshot being applied\n\tmsIndex := ms.snapshot.Metadata.Index\n\tsnapIndex := snap.Metadata.Index\n\t// During bootstrap, applications (e.g., etcd) may initialize only the\n\t// ConfState in the snapshot. In this case, both the snapshot index and\n\t// term are 0.\n\tif msIndex != 0 && msIndex >= snapIndex {\n\t\treturn ErrSnapOutOfDate\n\t}\n\n\tms.snapshot = snap\n\tms.ents = []pb.Entry{{Term: snap.Metadata.Term, Index: snap.Metadata.Index}}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// CreateSnapshot makes a snapshot which can be retrieved with Snapshot() and\n// can be used to reconstruct the state at that point.\n// If any configuration changes have been made since the last compaction,\n// the result of the last ApplyConfChange must be passed in.\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) CreateSnapshot(i uint64, cs *pb.ConfState, data []byte) (pb.Snapshot, error) {\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\tif i <= ms.snapshot.Metadata.Index {\n\t\treturn pb.Snapshot{}, ErrSnapOutOfDate\n\t}\n\n\toffset := ms.ents[0].Index\n\tif i > ms.lastIndex() {\n\t\tgetLogger().Panicf(\"snapshot %d is out of bound lastindex(%d)\", i, ms.lastIndex())\n\t}\n\n\tms.snapshot.Metadata.Index = i\n\tms.snapshot.Metadata.Term = ms.ents[i-offset].Term\n\tif cs != nil {\n\t\tms.snapshot.Metadata.ConfState = *cs\n\t}\n\tms.snapshot.Data = data\n\treturn ms.snapshot, nil\n}\n\n// Compact discards all log entries prior to compactIndex.\n// It is the application's responsibility to not attempt to compact an index\n// greater than raftLog.applied.\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) Compact(compactIndex uint64) error {\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\toffset := ms.ents[0].Index\n\tif compactIndex <= offset {\n\t\treturn ErrCompacted\n\t}\n\tif compactIndex > ms.lastIndex() {\n\t\tgetLogger().Panicf(\"compact %d is out of bound lastindex(%d)\", compactIndex, ms.lastIndex())\n\t}\n\n\ti := compactIndex - offset\n\t// NB: allocate a new slice instead of reusing the old ms.ents. Entries in\n\t// ms.ents are immutable, and can be referenced from outside MemoryStorage\n\t// through slices returned by ms.Entries().\n\tents := make([]pb.Entry, 1, uint64(len(ms.ents))-i)\n\tents[0].Index = ms.ents[i].Index\n\tents[0].Term = ms.ents[i].Term\n\tents = append(ents, ms.ents[i+1:]...)\n\tms.ents = ents\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// Append the new entries to storage.\n// TODO (xiangli): ensure the entries are continuous and\n// entries[0].Index > ms.entries[0].Index\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) Append(entries []pb.Entry) error {\n\tif len(entries) == 0 {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\n\tfirst := ms.firstIndex()\n\tlast := entries[0].Index + uint64(len(entries)) - 1\n\n\t// shortcut if there is no new entry.\n\tif last < first {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\t// truncate compacted entries\n\tif first > entries[0].Index {\n\t\tentries = entries[first-entries[0].Index:]\n\t}\n\n\toffset := entries[0].Index - ms.ents[0].Index\n\tswitch {\n\tcase uint64(len(ms.ents)) > offset:\n\t\t// NB: full slice expression protects ms.ents at index >= offset from\n\t\t// rewrites, as they may still be referenced from outside MemoryStorage.\n\t\tms.ents = append(ms.ents[:offset:offset], entries...)\n\tcase uint64(len(ms.ents)) == offset:\n\t\tms.ents = append(ms.ents, entries...)\n\tdefault:\n\t\tgetLogger().Panicf(\"missing log entry [last: %d, append at: %d]\",\n\t\t\tms.lastIndex(), entries[0].Index)\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":156,"oldLines":7,"newStart":156,"newLines":7,"lines":[" }"," "," // Term implements the Storage interface.","-func (ms *MemoryStorage) Term(i uint64) (uint64, error) {","+func (ms *InMemoryRaftLog) Term(i uint64) (uint64, error) {","   ms.Lock()","   defer ms.Unlock()","   ms.callStats.term++"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

1\n\n\t// shortcut if there is no new entry.\n\tif last < first {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\t// truncate compacted entries\n\tif first > entries[0].Index {\n\t\tentries = entries[first-entries[0].Index:]\n\t}\n\n\toffset := entries[0].Index - instance will\n// become inoperable and refuse to participate in elections; the\n// application is responsible for cleanup and recovery in this case.\ntype Storage interface {\n\t// TODO(tbg): split this into two interfaces, LogStorage and StateStorage.\n\n\t// InitialState returns the saved HardState and ConfState information.\n\tInitialState() (pb.HardState, pb.ConfState, error)\n\n\t// Entries returns a slice of consecutive log entries in the range [lo, hi),\n\t// starting from lo. The maxSize limits the total size of the log entries\n\t// returned, but Entries returns at least one entry if any.\n\t//\n\t// The caller of Entries owns the returned slice, and may append to it. The\n\t// individual entries in the slice must not be mutated, neither by the Storage\n\t// implementation nor the caller. Note that raft may forward these entries\n\t// back to the application via Ready struct, so the corresponding handler must\n\t// not mutate entries either (see comments in Ready struct).\n\t//\n\t// Since the caller may append to the returned slice, Storage implementation\n\t// must protect its state from corruption that such appends may cause. For\n\t// example, common ways to do so are:\n\t//  - allocate the slice before returning it (safest option),\n\t//  - return a slice protected by Go full slice expression, which causes\n\t//  copying on appends (see InMemoryRaftLog).\n\t//\n\t// Returns ErrCompacted if entry lo has been compacted, or ErrUnavailable if\n\t// encountered an unavailable entry in [lo, hi).\n\tEntries(lo, hi, maxSize uint64) ([]pb.Entry, error)\n\n\t// Term returns the term of entry i, which must be in the range\n\t// [FirstIndex()-1, LastIndex()]. The term of the entry before\n\t// FirstIndex is retained for matching purposes even though the\n\t// rest of that entry may not be available.\n\tTerm(i uint64) (uint64, error)\n\t// LastIndex returns the index of the last entry in the log.\n\tLastIndex() (uint64, error)\n\t// FirstIndex returns the index of the first log entry that is\n\t// possibly available via Entries (older entries have been incorporated\n\t// into the latest Snapshot; if storage only contains the dummy entry the\n\t// first log entry is not available).\n\tFirstIndex() (uint64, error)\n\t// Snapshot returns the most recent snapshot.\n\t// If snapshot is temporarily unavailable, it should return ErrSnapshotTemporarilyUnavailable,\n\t// so raft state machine could know that Storage needs some time to prepare\n\t// snapshot and call Snapshot later.\n\tSnapshot() (pb.Snapshot, error)\n}\n\ntype inMemStorageCallStats struct {\n\tinitialState, firstIndex, lastIndex, entries, term, snapshot int\n}\n\n// InMemoryRaftLog implements the Storage interface backed by an\n// in-memory array.\ntype InMemoryRaftLog struct {\n\t// Protects access to all fields. permissions and\n// limitations under the License.\n\npackage raft\n\nimport (\n\t\"errors\"\n\t\"sync\"\n\n\tpb \"go.etcd.io/raft/v3/raftpb\"\n)\n\n// ErrCompacted is returned by Storage.Entries/Compact when a requested\n// index is unavailable because it predates the last snapshot.\nvar ErrCompacted = errors.New(\"requested index is unavailable due to compaction\")\n\n// ErrSnapOutOfDate is returned by Storage.CreateSnapshot when a requested\n// index is older than the existing snapshot.\nvar ErrSnapOutOfDate = errors.New(\"requested index is older than the existing snapshot\")\n\n// ErrUnavailable is returned by Storage interface when the requested log entries\n// are unavailable.\nvar ErrUnavailable = errors.New(\"requested entry at index is unavailable\")\n\n// ErrSnapshotTemporarilyUnavailable is returned by the Storage interface when the required\n// snapshot is temporarily unavailable.\nvar ErrSnapshotTemporarilyUnavailable = errors.New(\"snapshot is temporarily unavailable\")\n\n// Storage is an interface that may be implemented by the application\n// to retrieve log entries from storage.\n//\n// If any Storage method returns an error, the raft instance will\n// become inoperable and refuse to participate in elections; the\n// application is responsible for cleanup and recovery in this case.\ntype Storage interface {\n\t// TODO(tbg): split this into two interfaces, LogStorage and StateStorage.\n\n\t// InitialState returns the saved HardState and ConfState information.\n\tInitialState() (pb.HardState, pb.ConfState, error)\n\n\t// Entries returns a slice of consecutive log entries in the range [lo, hi),\n\t// starting from lo. The maxSize limits the total size of the log entries\n\t// returned, but Entries returns at least one entry if any.\n\t//\n\t// The caller of Entries owns the returned slice, and may append to it. The\n\t// individual entries in the slice must not be mutated, neither by the Storage\n\t// implementation nor the caller. Note that raft may forward these entries\n\t// back to the application via Ready struct, so the corresponding handler must\n\t// not mutate entries either (see comments in Ready struct).\n\t//\n\t// Since the caller may append to the returned slice, Storage implementation\n\t// must protect its state from corruption that such appends may cause. For\n\t// example, common ways to do so are:\n\t//  - allocate the slice before returning it (safest option),\n\t//  - return a slice protected by Go full slice expression, which causes\n\t//  copying on appends (see InMemoryRaftLog).\n\t//\n\t// Returns ErrCompacted if entry lo has been compacted, or ErrUnavailable if\n\t// encountered an unavailable entry in [lo, hi).\n\tEntries(lo, hi, maxSize uint64) ([]pb.Entry, error)\n\n\t// Term returns the term of entry i, which must be in the range\n\t// [FirstIndex()-1, LastIndex()]. The term of the entry before\n\t// FirstIndex is retained for matching purposes even though the\n\t// rest of that entry may not be available.\n\tTerm(i uint64) (uint64, error)\n\t// LastIndex returns the index of the last entry in the log.\n\tLastIndex() (uint64, error)\n\t// FirstIndex returns the index of the first log entry that is\n\t// possibly available via Entries (older entries have been incorporated\n\t// into the latest Snapshot; if storage only contains the dummy entry the\n\t// first log entry is not available).\n\tFirstIndex() (uint64, error)\n\t// Snapshot returns the most recent snapshot.\n\t// If snapshot is temporarily unavailable, it should return ErrSnapshotTemporarilyUnavailable,\n\t// so raft state machine could know that Storage needs some time to prepare\n\t// snapshot and call Snapshot later.\n\tSnapshot() (pb.Snapshot, error)\n}\n\ntype inMemStorageCallStats struct {\n\tinitialState, firstIndex, lastIndex, entries, term, snapshot int\n}\n\n// InMemoryRaftLog implements the Storage interface backed by an\n// in-memory array.\ntype InMemoryRaftLog struct {\n\t// Protects access to all fields. Most methods of InMemoryRaftLog are\n\t// run on the raft goroutine, but Append() is run on an application\n\t// goroutine.\n\tsync.Mutex\n\n\thardState pb.HardState\n\tsnapshot  pb.Snapshot\n\t// ents[i] has raft log position i+snapshot.Metadata.Index\n\tents []pb.Entry\n\n\tcallStats inMemStorageCallStats\n}\n\n// NewInMemoryRaftLog creates an empty InMemoryRaftLog.\nfunc NewInMemoryRaftLog() *InMemoryRaftLog {\n\treturn &InMemoryRaftLog{\n\t\t// When starting from scratch populate the list with a dummy entry at term zero.\n\t\tents: make([]pb.Entry, 1),\n\t}\n}\n\n// InitialState implements the Storage interface.\nfunc (ms *InMemoryRaftLog) InitialState() (pb.HardState, pb.ConfState, error) {\n\tms.callStats.initialState++\n\treturn ms.hardState, ms.snapshot.Metadata.ConfState, nil\n}\n\n// SetHardState saves the current HardState.\nfunc (ms *InMemoryRaftLog) SetHardState(st pb.HardState) error {\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\tms.hardState = st\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// Entries implements the Storage interface.\nfunc (ms *InMemoryRaftLog) Entries(lo, hi, maxSize uint64) ([]pb.Entry, error) {\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\tms.callStats.entries++\n\toffset := ms.ents[0].Index\n\tif lo <= offset {\n\t\treturn nil, ErrCompacted\n\t}\n\tif hi > ms.lastIndex()+1 {\n\t\tgetLogger().Panicf(\"entries' hi(%d) is out of bound lastindex(%d)\", hi, ms.lastIndex())\n\t}\n\t// only contains dummy entries.\n\tif len(ms.ents) == 1 {\n\t\treturn nil, ErrUnavailable\n\t}\n\n\tents := limitSize(ms.ents[lo-offset:hi-offset], entryEncodingSize(maxSize))\n\t// NB: use the full slice expression to limit what the caller can do with the\n\t// returned slice. For example, an append will reallocate and copy this slice\n\t// instead of corrupting the neighbouring ms.ents.\n\treturn ents[:len(ents):len(ents)], nil\n}\n\n// Term implements the Storage interface.\nfunc (ms *InMemoryRaftLog) Term(i uint64) (uint64, error) {\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\tms.callStats.term++\n\toffset := ms.ents[0].Index\n\tif i < offset {\n\t\treturn 0, ErrCompacted\n\t}\n\tif int(i-offset) >= len(ms.ents) {\n\t\treturn 0, ErrUnavailable\n\t}\n\treturn ms.ents[i-offset].Term, nil\n}\n\n// LastIndex implements the Storage interface.\nfunc (ms *InMemoryRaftLog) LastIndex() (uint64, error) {\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\tms.callStats.lastIndex++\n\treturn ms.lastIndex(), nil\n}\n\nfunc (ms *InMemoryRaftLog) lastIndex() uint64 {\n\treturn ms.ents[0].Index + uint64(len(ms.ents)) - 1\n}\n\n// FirstIndex implements the Storage interface.\nfunc (ms *InMemoryRaftLog) FirstIndex() (uint64, error) {\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\tms.callStats.firstIndex++\n\treturn ms.firstIndex(), nil\n}\n\nfunc (ms *InMemoryRaftLog) firstIndex() uint64 {\n\treturn ms.ents[0].Index + 1\n}\n\n// Snapshot implements the Storage interface.\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) Snapshot() (pb.Snapshot, error) {\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\tms.callStats.snapshot++\n\treturn ms.snapshot, nil\n}\n\n// ApplySnapshot overwrites the contents of this Storage object with\n// those of the given snapshot.\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) ApplySnapshot(snap pb.Snapshot) error {\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\n\t//handle check for old snapshot being applied\n\tmsIndex := ms.snapshot.Metadata.Index\n\tsnapIndex := snap.Metadata.Index\n\t// During bootstrap, applications (e.g., etcd) may initialize only the\n\t// ConfState in the snapshot. In this case, both the snapshot index and\n\t// term are 0.\n\tif msIndex != 0 && msIndex >= snapIndex {\n\t\treturn ErrSnapOutOfDate\n\t}\n\n\tms.snapshot = snap\n\tms.ents = []pb.Entry{{Term: snap.Metadata.Term, Index: snap.Metadata.Index}}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// CreateSnapshot makes a snapshot which can be retrieved with Snapshot() and\n// can be used to reconstruct the state at that point.\n// If any configuration changes have been made since the last compaction,\n// the result of the last ApplyConfChange must be passed in.\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) CreateSnapshot(i uint64, cs *pb.ConfState, data []byte) (pb.Snapshot, error) {\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\tif i <= ms.snapshot.Metadata.Index {\n\t\treturn pb.Snapshot{}, ErrSnapOutOfDate\n\t}\n\n\toffset := ms.ents[0].Index\n\tif i > ms.lastIndex() {\n\t\tgetLogger().Panicf(\"snapshot %d is out of bound lastindex(%d)\", i, ms.lastIndex())\n\t}\n\n\tms.snapshot.Metadata.Index = i\n\tms.snapshot.Metadata.Term = ms.ents[i-offset].Term\n\tif cs != nil {\n\t\tms.snapshot.Metadata.ConfState = *cs\n\t}\n\tms.snapshot.Data = data\n\treturn ms.snapshot, nil\n}\n\n// Compact discards all log entries prior to compactIndex.\n// It is the application's responsibility to not attempt to compact an index\n// greater than raftLog.applied.\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) Compact(compactIndex uint64) error {\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\toffset := ms.ents[0].Index\n\tif compactIndex <= offset {\n\t\treturn ErrCompacted\n\t}\n\tif compactIndex > ms.lastIndex() {\n\t\tgetLogger().Panicf(\"compact %d is out of bound lastindex(%d)\", compactIndex, ms.lastIndex())\n\t}\n\n\ti := compactIndex - offset\n\t// NB: allocate a new slice instead of reusing the old ms.ents. Entries in\n\t// ms.ents are immutable, and can be referenced from outside MemoryStorage\n\t// through slices returned by ms.Entries().\n\tents := make([]pb.Entry, 1, uint64(len(ms.ents))-i)\n\tents[0].Index = ms.ents[i].Index\n\tents[0].Term = ms.ents[i].Term\n\tents = append(ents, ms.ents[i+1:]...)\n\tms.ents = ents\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// Append the new entries to storage.\n// TODO (xiangli): ensure the entries are continuous and\n// entries[0].Index > ms.entries[0].Index\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) Append(entries []pb.Entry) error {\n\tif len(entries) == 0 {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\n\tfirst := ms.firstIndex()\n\tlast := entries[0].Index + uint64(len(entries)) - 1\n\n\t// shortcut if there is no new entry.\n\tif last < first {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\t// truncate compacted entries\n\tif first > entries[0].Index {\n\t\tentries = entries[first-entries[0].Index:]\n\t}\n\n\toffset := entries[0].Index - ms.ents[0].Index\n\tswitch {\n\tcase uint64(len(ms.ents)) > offset:\n\t\t// NB: full slice expression protects ms.ents at index >= offset from\n\t\t// rewrites, as they may still be referenced from outside MemoryStorage.\n\t\tms.ents = append(ms.ents[:offset:offset], entries...)\n\tcase uint64(len(ms.ents)) == offset:\n\t\tms.ents = append(ms.ents, entries...)\n\tdefault:\n\t\tgetLogger().Panicf(\"missing log entry [last: %d, append at: %d]\",\n\t\t\tms.lastIndex(), entries[0].Index)\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":195,"oldLines":7,"newStart":195,"newLines":7,"lines":[" }"," "," // Snapshot implements the Storage interface.","-func (ms *MemoryStorage) Snapshot() (pb.Snapshot, error) {","+func (ms *InMemoryRaftLog) Snapshot() (pb.Snapshot, error) {","   ms.Lock()","   defer ms.Unlock()","   ms.callStats.snapshot++"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

For example, an append will reallocate and copy this slice\n\t// instead of corrupting the neighbouring ms.ents.\n\treturn ents[:len(ents):len(ents)], nil\n}\n\n// Term implements the Storage interface.\nfunc (ms *InMemoryRaftLog) Term(i uint64) (uint64, error) {\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\tms.callStats.term++\n\toffset := ms.ents[0].Index\n\tif i < offset {\n\t\treturn 0, ErrCompacted\n\t}\n\tif int(i-offset) >= len(ms.ents) {\n\t\treturn 0, ErrUnavailable\n\t}\n\treturn ms.ents[i-offset].Term, nil\n}\n\n// LastIndex implements the Storage interface.\nfunc (ms *InMemoryRaftLog) LastIndex() (uint64, error) {\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\tms.callStats.lastIndex++\n\treturn ms.lastIndex(), nil\n}\n\nfunc (ms *InMemoryRaftLog) lastIndex() uint64 {\n\treturn ms.ents[0].Index + uint64(len(ms.ents)) - 1\n}\n\n// FirstIndex implements the Storage interface.\nfunc (ms *InMemoryRaftLog) FirstIndex() (uint64, error) {\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\tms.callStats.firstIndex++\n\treturn ms.firstIndex(), nil\n}\n\nfunc (ms *InMemoryRaftLog) firstIndex() uint64 {\n\treturn ms.ents[0].Index + 1\n}\n\n// Snapshot implements the Storage interface.\nfunc (ms *InMemoryRaftLog) Snapshot() (pb.Snapshot, error) {\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\tms.callStats.snapshot++\n\treturn ms.snapshot, nil\n}\n\n// ApplySnapshot overwrites the contents of this Storage object with\n// those of the given snapshot.\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) ApplySnapshot(snap pb.Snapshot) error {\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\n\t//handle check for old snapshot being applied\n\tmsIndex := ms.snapshot.Metadata.Index\n\tsnapIndex := snap.Metadata.Index\n\t// During bootstrap, applications (e.g., etcd) may initialize only the\n\t// ConfState in the snapshot. In this case, both the snapshot index and\n\t// term are 0.\n\tif msIndex != 0 && msIndex >= snapIndex {\n\t\treturn ErrSnapOutOfDate\n\t}\n\n\tms.snapshot = snap\n\tms.ents = []pb.Entry{{Term: snap.Metadata.Term, Index: snap.Metadata.Index}}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// CreateSnapshot makes a snapshot which can be retrieved with Snapshot() and\n// can be used to reconstruct the state at that point.\n// If any configuration changes have been made since the last compaction,\n// the result of the last ApplyConfChange must be passed in.\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) CreateSnapshot(i uint64, cs *pb.ConfState, data []byte) (pb.Snapshot, error) {\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\tif i <= ms.snapshot.Metadata.Index {\n\t\treturn pb.Snapshot{}, ErrSnapOutOfDate\n\t}\n\n\toffset := ms.ents[0].Index\n\tif i > ms.lastIndex() {\n\t\tgetLogger().Panicf(\"snapshot %d is out of bound lastindex(%d)\", i, ms.lastIndex())\n\t}\n\n\tms.snapshot.Metadata.Index = i\n\tms.snapshot.Metadata.Term = ms.ents[i-offset].Term\n\tif cs != nil {\n\t\tms.snapshot.Metadata.ConfState = *cs\n\t}\n\tms.snapshot.Data = data\n\treturn ms.snapshot, nil\n}\n\n// Compact discards all log entries prior to compactIndex.\n// It is the application's responsibility to not attempt to compact an index\n// greater than raftLog.applied.\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) Compact(compactIndex uint64) error {\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\toffset := ms.ents[0].Index\n\tif compactIndex <= offset {\n\t\treturn ErrCompacted\n\t}\n\tif compactIndex > ms.lastIndex() {\n\t\tgetLogger().Panicf(\"compact %d is out of bound lastindex(%d)\", compactIndex, ms.lastIndex())\n\t}\n\n\ti := compactIndex - offset\n\t// NB: allocate a new slice instead of reusing the old ms.ents. Entries in\n\t// ms.ents are immutable, and can be referenced from outside MemoryStorage\n\t// through slices returned by ms.Entries().\n\tents := make([]pb.Entry, 1, uint64(len(ms.ents))-i)\n\tents[0].Index = ms.ents[i].Index\n\tents[0].Term = ms.ents[i].Term\n\tents = append(ents, ms.ents[i+1:]...)\n\tms.ents = ents\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// Append the new entries to storage.\n// TODO (xiangli): ensure the entries are continuous and\n// entries[0].Index > ms.entries[0].Index\nfunc (ms *MemoryStorage) Append(entries []pb.Entry) error {\n\tif len(entries) == 0 {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\tms.Lock()\n\tdefer ms.Unlock()\n\n\tfirst := ms.firstIndex()\n\tlast := entries[0].Index + uint64(len(entries)) - 1\n\n\t// shortcut if there is no new entry.\n\tif last < first {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\t// truncate compacted entries\n\tif first > entries[0].Index {\n\t\tentries = entries[first-entries[0].Index:]\n\t}\n\n\toffset := entries[0].Index - ms.ents[0].Index\n\tswitch {\n\tcase uint64(len(ms.ents)) > offset:\n\t\t// NB: full slice expression protects ms.ents at index >= offset from\n\t\t// rewrites, as they may still be referenced from outside MemoryStorage.\n\t\tms.ents = append(ms.ents[:offset:offset], entries...)\n\tcase uint64(len(ms.ents)) == offset:\n\t\tms.ents = append(ms.ents, entries...)\n\tdefault:\n\t\tgetLogger().Panicf(\"missing log entry [last: %d, append at: %d]\",\n\t\t\tms.lastIndex(), entries[0].Index)\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":204,"oldLines":7,"newStart":204,"newLines":7,"lines":[" "," // ApplySnapshot overwrites the contents of this Storage object with"," // those of the given snapshot.","-func (ms *MemoryStorage) ApplySnapshot(snap pb.Snapshot) error {","+func (ms *InMemoryRaftLog) ApplySnapshot(snap pb.Snapshot) error {","   ms.Lock()","   defer ms.Unlock()"," "]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}
